stories.headline: Bitcoin slides to $76,340 ahead of Fed as $80K rejection draws third straight failure
stories.tldr: BTC opened 1.3% lower at $76,340 Wednesday, with Coinbase premium turning negative as traders brace for the Fed decision later today.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Repeated $80K failures plus weakening US demand sets up a deeper flush if Powell signals no near-term rate cuts.
the_details: BTC twice rejected at $80,000 over the last week before sliding to $76,340 open on April 29.
Coinbase premium index has flipped negative, signaling US institutional buyers stepping back.
ETH, XRP and SOL all down 1–4% on the week, with XRP taking the worst hit.

stories.headline: SEC-CFTC joint taxonomy carves BTC, ETH, SOL, XRP, LINK as digital commodities
stories.tldr: Joint SEC-CFTC guidance sorts crypto into five buckets and names the top five large-caps as digital commodities, ending years of jurisdictional ambiguity.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A clean commodities label for ETH, SOL, XRP and LINK clears the runway for futures, ETFs and bank custody at scale.
the_details: Five categories: digital commodities, digital collectibles, digital tools, stablecoins, digital securities.
FDIC dropped proposed GENIUS Act stablecoin rules April 7; FinCEN/OFAC followed with PPSI AML rules April 8.
CFTC Innovation Task Force named April 10 to write the framework for crypto, AI and prediction markets.

stories.headline: April crypto hack toll hits $606M across 12 incidents in 18 days; Lazarus dominates
stories.tldr: DeFi has bled $606M to exploits in just 18 days of April, with North Korea's Lazarus Group fingerprints on the two biggest hits.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A $770M YTD security tab plus state-actor attribution will harden insurer pricing and accelerate the institutional flight from cross-chain bridges.
the_details: Kelp DAO drained $292M April 19 via misconfigured LayerZero bridge minting 116,500 unbacked rsETH.
Drift Protocol lost $285M April 1 via fake CVT token, oracle manipulation and compromised admin key.
2026 YTD DeFi hack losses now near $770M, on pace to eclipse 2024's full-year total.

stories.headline: CLARITY Act markup slips into May with no firm date, killing April catalyst
stories.tldr: The CLARITY Act markup that traders pegged as April's main upside catalyst has been pushed to May with no confirmed date set.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: With CLARITY off the table, BTC loses its main near-term legislative tailwind right as macro headwinds re-enter the room.
the_details: Bill drafted to settle SEC vs CFTC jurisdictional lines for digital assets.
April markup window has formally closed with no May date confirmed by House leadership.
Traders had front-run the markup; positioning is now unwinding into a thinner tape.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan debut as Consensus Miami sponsors as Wall Street books $10T into May 5
stories.tldr: Consensus Miami's institutional share nearly doubles to ~35% of 15,000 attendees, with TradFi heavyweights now headline sponsors.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: $10T in AUM physically showing up validates that the institutional thesis is past pilot stage and into allocation mode.
the_details: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan are debut sponsors at the May 5–7 Miami event.
CFTC Chair Michael Selig, Sen. Ashley Moody and White House's Patrick Witt attend a Consensus event for the first time.
Conference expects 15,000+ attendees; institutional share rises to ~35% representing ~$10T in AUM.
